npcs.name: Old Man
npcs.personality: A cryptic, slightly unhinged hermit who speaks in riddles and dark humor. He knows far more than he lets on, but extracting useful information requires patience and the right conversation choices.
npcs.defaultGreeting: The old man stares at you with unsettling intensity. He might be wise, or he might just be old.

npcs.name: Stone Merchant
npcs.personality: A half-petrified merchant frozen mid-sales-pitch. Eternally optimistic despite literally turning to stone. Can still talk, slowly, through stone lips. His right arm and most of his torso are grey stone, but his left arm gestures enthusiastically.
npcs.defaultGreeting: The merchant's one mobile eye tracks your approach. His stone lips crack into something that was probably meant to be a welcoming smile.

npcs.name: Bertram the Bridge Troll
npcs.personality: A friendly, well-read troll who is contractually obligated to block the bridge. Apologetic about it. Has a book club, a mortgage, and very little tolerance for rudeness.
npcs.defaultGreeting: A large, surprisingly well-groomed troll adjusts his reading glasses and looks up from his book. He has the patient expression of a professional who takes his work seriously.

npcs.name: Ghost King
npcs.personality: Sardonic dead monarch. Bitter about dying from an olive. Self-aware about his failures. Dry wit. He's had centuries to think about his mistakes and has catalogued every one.
npcs.defaultGreeting: The translucent figure of King Mortimer hovers above the throne, radiating spectral irritation and the faint smell of ectoplasm.

npcs.name: Martha the Cook
npcs.personality: Practical, no-nonsense, exasperated by everyone. Has kept the castle running single-handedly. Best character in the game and she knows it. Wields a cleaver with authority.
npcs.defaultGreeting: Martha the Cook looks up from her chopping block with the expression of someone who has been the only competent person in a building for far too long.

npcs.name: The Clerk
npcs.personality: Immortal bureaucrat with zero personality and infinite patience. Speaks exclusively in administrative jargon. Secretly the original curse-caster but does not remember. His entire identity is procedure.
npcs.defaultGreeting: The Clerk regards you over his spectacles with the expression of someone who has processed ten million applicants and found fault with nine million nine hundred and ninety-nine thousand of them.

npcs.name: Patient Ghost
npcs.personality: A ghost who has been waiting in the queue for 200 years. Remarkably patient. Has numbered every crack in the ceiling. Cheerful despite circumstances. Finds waiting meditative.
npcs.defaultGreeting: A translucent figure waves from a stone bench. He's been sitting there for 200 years and seems genuinely happy to see someone new.

npcs.name: Forge Spirit
npcs.personality: Ancient dwarven smith. Gruff but fair. Respects craftsmanship above all else. Will only help if you show proper respect for the forge. Speaks rarely, but when he does, it matters.
npcs.defaultGreeting: The spectral dwarven figure opens one eye, evaluates you with the critical gaze of a master craftsman, and closes it again. You have not yet earned his attention.

npcs.name: Captain Aldric
npcs.personality: Half-petrified guard captain. Stoic, dutiful, slowly turning to stone but still giving orders. Deeply loyal to the kingdom. His right side is stone, his left still flesh. He refuses to abandon his post.
npcs.defaultGreeting: Captain Aldric stands at attention -- or as close to attention as a half-petrified man can manage. His one living eye fixes on you with the intensity of someone who has been waiting for exactly this moment.

npcs.name: Mirror Spirit
npcs.personality: An entity that lives in the Hall of Mirrors. Shows truth through reflections. Speaks in a chorus of reflected voices. Neither kind nor cruel -- simply honest, which is often worse.
npcs.defaultGreeting: The face in the mirror watches you approach. It is not your face. It is something older, wiser, and considerably less impressed.

npcs.name: Wizard Marlowe
npcs.personality: The ghost of the wizard who cast the curse. Doesn't remember casting it. Thinks he's still alive. Very confused. Mutters about reagent prices and parking regulations. Harmless but unhelpful.
npcs.defaultGreeting: A spectral figure drifts between the shelves, muttering about the rising cost of bat guano. He startles when he sees you. 'A customer! No, wait. A visitor? I don't get visitors. Or do I? I can't remember.'
